description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical base constructed from stacked, gray stone bricks. The well is topped with a pitched, terracotta-tiled roof supported by two wooden posts, providing shelter from above. A wooden bucket is suspended by a rope from a horizontal wooden beam, which is operated by a metal crank handle attached to one side. The interior of the well is open, allowing access to the water below, and the structure is robust and utilitarian, typical of wells found in parks or older urban neighborhoods. The stonework appears rough and weathered, while the roof tiles and wood have a more finished appearance. There are no visible decorative elements, and the design prioritizes function and durability.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a terracotta-tiled roof, wooden supports, and a crank-operated bucket. The structure is sturdy and functional, with a rustic, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden supports, tiled roof, and the bucket hanging centrally with the crank handle visible on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pitched roof profile, the wooden support structure, and the bucket hanging inside the well.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the crank handle now on the left and the bucket still centrally suspended.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the roof and support posts, with the bucket partially visible inside the well.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket is suspended by a rope from a horizontal beam, operated by a metal crank handle on the side.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a cylindrical stone wall approximately 1 meter high and topped with a pitched roof supported by wooden posts.
